value_picks.name: ServiceNow, Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF 5.108B / mcap 108.78B = 4.70% FCF yield, in the 4-7% borderline band.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 20.98 vs implied PE 27.35 (median target 137.5 / fwd EPS 5.027) = 23.3% discount, within 15-30% borderline.

drawdown.detail: Down 50.1% from 52w high of 211.48; well past the 35% pass threshold.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash positive: cash 5.182B - debt 2.431B = +2.75B. D/E 20.7 is low; current ratio 0.845 is soft but net-cash status passes outright.

institutional.detail: Institutions 88.6% (>75%) and recommendation_mean 1.44 (<2.5); 43 of 48 ratings buy/strong-buy.

ai_exposure.detail: Workflow automation and Now Assist / agentic-AI products position NOW to monetize enterprise AI adoption directly; AI raises seat value rather than threatening the platform.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Short interest 5.93% with shares short up 24.0% MoM (61.0M vs 49.2M) at only 2.05 days-to-cover — modest, fast-to-cover bearish positioning into the drawdown.

catalysts_general: Trading 50% below 52w high while revenue still growing 22.1% YoY and PEG 0.99.
Forward PE 20.98 vs trailing 62.8 implies large embedded earnings step-up (fwd EPS 5.03 vs trailing 1.68).
Net cash balance sheet (+2.75B) with 76.6% gross margins funds buybacks/M&A without dilution risk.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Multiple compresses toward low-target zone on a soft enterprise IT-spend quarter.
Retest of 52w low / analyst low target (85) in a broad software de-rating.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near the 52w low and just above the 85 analyst floor, fwd PE ~17.5x — net-cash GARP name at a trough multiple with growth intact.

comparables.note: Larger app-suite peer; NOW grows faster at a similar multiple.
Closest enterprise-workflow comp; similar GARP profile.

value_picks.analyst_take: Highest-quality name in the set: 22% revenue growth, 76% gross margin, net cash, and near-unanimous analyst conviction (rec 1.44), yet down 50% from highs. Value metrics are GARP not deep-value (P/S 7.8), but the score of 4.5 and trough multiple make it the cleanest BUY here.

value_picks.name: Ingersoll Rand Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF 1.035B / mcap 30.35B = 3.41% FCF yield, below the 4% threshold.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 20.18 vs implied PE 23.93 (median target 92 / fwd EPS 3.844) = 15.7% discount, just inside the 15-30% band.

drawdown.detail: Down 23.2% from 52w high of 100.96 — in the 20-35% borderline zone; least dislocated name in the set.

balance_sheet.detail: Net debt (cash 1.29B - debt 4.95B = -3.66B), but D/E 48.4 (<50) AND current ratio 2.227 (>1.2) satisfies the pass condition.

dividend.detail: Dividend yield 0.11% (>0) with payout 5.4% (<90%) — sustainable but token; borderline.

ai_exposure.detail: Core air/gas compression and vacuum machinery is mechanical; AI is peripheral, though data-center cooling and clean-energy capex offer an indirect demand tailwind.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Short interest low at 4.38% (essentially flat, +2.0% MoM); no notable positioning signal. IR is itself a serial acquirer — bolt-on M&A is the structural growth lever.

catalysts_general: 20-day price +10.8% — strongest near-term momentum in the set, rebounding off the 50-day.
PEG 0.71 with 7.6% revenue growth; high-quality compounder but only modestly discounted.
Net debt 3.66B against EBITDA 2.02B (~1.8x) — manageable leverage with a 2.23 current ratio.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Mean-reversion of the recent 10.8% pop back toward the 50-day in an industrials pullback.
Retest of 52w low on a cyclical/PMI scare.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Closer to the 52w low and below the 80 analyst floor, fwd PE ~18x — only at a deeper discount does the low FCF yield and 23% drawdown justify a value entry.

comparables.note: Diversified industrial peer at a slightly lower multiple.
Similar serial-acquirer compounder model; richer multiple.

value_picks.analyst_take: A genuinely good industrial compounder, but the weakest value setup here: lowest drawdown (23%), sub-4% FCF yield, and only a 15.7% discount to consensus. Quality earns a WATCHLIST, but wait for a pullback toward the low-70s before paying up.

value_picks.name: PayPal Holdings, Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF 4.084B / mcap 37.83B = 10.79% FCF yield — comfortably above 7%.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 7.45 vs implied PE 8.34 (median target 48 / fwd EPS 5.758) = only 10.6% discount; the stock is already cheap vs target, so the gap is <15%.

drawdown.detail: Down 46.2% from 52w high of 79.50 — past the 35% threshold.

balance_sheet.detail: Net debt (cash 9.34B - debt 11.67B = -2.33B); D/E 58.3 (in 50-150 band) AND current ratio 1.259 (>0.8) = borderline.

dividend.detail: Dividend yield 1.35% (>0) with payout 5.25% (<90%) — newly initiated, well-covered, but yield <2%; borderline.

institutional.detail: Institutions 80.1% (>60%) but recommendation_mean 2.88 (<3.0, not <2.5); 31 of 43 ratings are holds. Borderline.

ai_exposure.detail: Mixed: agentic-AI commerce and one-click AI checkout could disintermediate the branded button (threat), but PayPal's AI-driven fraud/risk and Venmo/Fastlane tools are a tailwind. Net neutral.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Short interest 6.26% with shares short up 15.7% MoM (49.5M vs 42.8M) — building bearish bets, but the 10.8% FCF yield funds heavy buybacks that shrink the float.

catalysts_general: Trades at 7.45x forward earnings with a 10.8% FCF yield and 25.1% ROE — classic washed-out deep-value profile.
Buyback engine: 4.08B FCF on a 37.8B cap retires meaningful float annually at depressed prices.
Negative earnings growth (-6.2%) is the bear case the low multiple already prices.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Retest of 52w low as branded-checkout share loss / take-rate compression persists.
Analyst low-target value-trap case — margins erode and the multiple stays single-digit.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just above the 38.46 52w low, fwd PE ~6.8x and FCF yield ~11.5% — buying maximum pessimism in a cash machine the street has written off.

comparables.note: Fiserv trades at ~2x PYPL's multiple on similar payments exposure — gap reflects PYPL's narrative discount.
Global Payments similarly de-rated; PYPL has the better FCF yield and balance sheet.

value_picks.analyst_take: The textbook deep-value setup this framework exists to catch: 7.45x forward earnings, ~11% FCF yield, 46% drawdown, aggressive buybacks, with the street firmly neutral (31 holds). The risk is a value trap on declining earnings, but at this price the cash generation and washed-out sentiment make it a BUY for a value mandate.

value_picks.name: lululemon athletica inc.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 10.26 vs implied PE 10.99 (median target 127 / fwd EPS 11.553) = only 6.7% discount; consensus target sits barely above spot.

drawdown.detail: Down 53.2% from 52w high of 252.24 — deepest drawdown in the set.

balance_sheet.detail: Net debt small (cash 1.51B - debt 2.14B = -0.62B), but D/E 44.3 (<50) AND current ratio 2.226 (>1.2) passes; ROE 32%.

institutional.detail: Institutions 82.6% (>75%) but recommendation_mean 3.03 (>3.0) fails outright; 30 of 33 ratings holds plus a sell and a strong-sell.

ai_exposure.detail: Premium athletic apparel demand is brand- and fashion-driven; AI is peripheral, useful only at the margin for inventory/supply-chain and personalization.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Short interest 6.68% with shares short up 30.4% MoM (7.23M vs 5.54M) — the largest short build in the set, consistent with a falling-knife / deteriorating-fundamentals narrative.

catalysts_general: Down 53% from highs and 24.5% in 50 days — a once-premium grower now at 10x earnings with 55.7% gross margin and 32% ROE.
Earnings growth -35% YoY is the active deterioration driving the de-rating; revenue still +4.3%.
Strong balance sheet (CR 2.23, near-zero net debt) gives runway to defend the brand through the slowdown.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Retest of 52w low if a guide-down confirms US comp weakness.
Analyst low-target case — growth stalls and LULU re-rates to a no-growth apparel multiple.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Below the 109 52w low into the high-double-digit-discount zone, fwd PE ~9x — only worth catching once price stabilizes or a quarter shows the earnings decline bottoming.

comparables.note: Larger athletic peer at a far higher multiple despite its own slowdown — LULU is cheaper but shrinking faster.
Higher-growth footwear comp; LULU's discount reflects negative earnings momentum.

value_picks.analyst_take: Cheap, cash-generative, and 53% off its high with an elite brand and pristine balance sheet — but -35% earnings growth and a 3.03 analyst mean (mostly holds, a sell and a strong-sell) say the market sees a structural slowdown, not a dip. WATCHLIST until the earnings trajectory stabilizes; this is a knife still falling.

value_picks.name: GoDaddy Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF 1.348B / mcap 10.47B = 12.87% FCF yield — the highest in the set.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 7.34 vs implied PE 9.28 (median target 100 / fwd EPS 10.773) = 20.9% discount, inside the 15-30% band.

drawdown.detail: Down 56.5% from 52w high of 181.49 — past the 35% threshold.

balance_sheet.detail: D/E 1621.6 (>150) AND current ratio 0.671 (<0.8) both trip the fail condition. Equity is tiny (book value 1.79) from years of buybacks; net debt 2.54B. Optically distressed though FCF-covered.

institutional.detail: Institutions 105.0% (>75%), but recommendation_mean is absent in the input; using analyst distribution, buy ratio is 8 of 16 (50%) with zero sells. Scored borderline on available fields; rec_mean unavailable.

ai_exposure.detail: Two-sided: GoDaddy's Airo AI website/marketing tools lift ARPU and conversion (tailwind), but generative-AI site builders also commoditize DIY web presence and invite competition (threat). Net neutral.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Short interest 7.37% with shares short up 22.3% MoM (7.25M vs 5.93M) at 2.52 days-to-cover — rising bearish positioning, partly reflecting the optically extreme leverage.

catalysts_general: Cheapest forward multiple paired with the richest FCF yield: 7.34x fwd PE, 12.9% FCF yield, down 56% from highs.
24.7% operating margins and 1.35B FCF easily service the 3.85B debt (~2.9x EBITDA) — leverage is buyback-driven, not distress.
20-day price -13.7% — recent sharp leg down creates the entry but reflects negative momentum.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Retest of 52w low if subscriber/bookings growth disappoints.
Multiple compresses further as AI-commoditization fears weigh on the core hosting moat.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near the 52w low and just below the 83 analyst floor, fwd PE ~6.7x and FCF yield ~13.5% — strong free-cash compounder, but size the position for the optical balance-sheet risk.

comparables.note: Website-builder peer at ~3x GDDY's multiple — GDDY is far cheaper on FCF.
Squarespace comparable; GDDY trades at a deep FCF-yield discount to both.

value_picks.analyst_take: Best FCF yield (12.9%) and lowest multiple in the set, 56% off its high, with a high-margin recurring revenue base. The catch is a balance sheet that screens as distressed (D/E 1622, CR 0.67) — though that is buyback-engineered negative equity, not insolvency, and FCF covers debt ~2.9x. WATCHLIST: attractive cash machine, but the leverage optics and -13.7% momentum argue for a low-70s entry and modest sizing.

value_picks.name: Fair Isaac Corporation

fcf_yield.detail: FCF 0.731B / mcap 27.60B = 2.65% FCF yield, below 4%; FCF understates owner earnings for this asset-light model but still fails the test.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 21.87 vs implied PE 28.40 (median target 1546 / fwd EPS 54.431) = 23.0% discount, inside the 15-30% band.

drawdown.detail: Down 40.4% from 52w high of 1998.01 — past the 35% threshold.

balance_sheet.detail: Not net cash (cash 0.22B - debt 3.66B = -3.44B net debt); debt_to_equity is absent because book equity is negative (-90.22, buyback-driven), so D/E is not meaningful. Current ratio 2.223 (>0.8) is strong and net debt is only ~12% of mcap (~3.1x EBITDA). Scored borderline on available fields.

institutional.detail: Institutions 95.4% (>75%) and recommendation_mean 1.81 (<2.5); 16 of 21 ratings buy/strong-buy.

ai_exposure.detail: FICO's business is predictive analytics and ML-based decisioning (Scores + Platform); AI strengthens its fraud, credit, and decision-software franchise rather than threatening it. The credit-score monopoly is regulatory/data-moated against AI disruption.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Short interest highest in the set at 9.65% with shares short up 20.6% MoM (1.88M vs 1.56M) at 5.15 days-to-cover — meaningful, slow-to-cover bearish positioning on valuation/mortgage-cyclicality concerns.

catalysts_general: Down 40% from highs while revenue grew 38.7% and earnings 69% YoY — a high-quality compounder on sale.
Elite economics: 84.2% gross, 58.2% operating margins; the Scores segment is a price-inelastic toll on US credit.
Still not cheap on absolute terms (P/S 12.2, fwd PE 21.9) and FCF yield only 2.65% — the de-rating reflects mortgage-volume and pricing-scrutiny risk.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Further multiple compression on score-pricing political scrutiny or a mortgage-origination slowdown.
Retest of 52w low if growth decelerates toward the analyst low target (707).

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near the 52w low, fwd PE ~17x — for a 38%-revenue-grower with 58% operating margins, mid-teens forward earnings is where the quality outweighs the low headline FCF yield.

comparables.note: S&P Global is the closest data/analytics-moat peer at a higher multiple than FICO post-drawdown.
Moody's similarly moated; FICO now trades at a notable discount to both on forward PE despite faster growth.

value_picks.analyst_take: The highest-quality compounder in the group — 38.7% revenue growth, 58% operating margin, a monopoly-grade scores franchise, down 40% with strong analyst backing (rec 1.81). But it fails on value mechanics: 2.65% FCF yield and a still-rich P/S of 12.2, and the highest short interest in the set. WATCHLIST — a great business at a better-but-not-cheap price; accumulate closer to the 900s.

growth_picks.name: Visa Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: revenue_growth 17.1% sits in the 15-30% band — solid for a mature network but not high-growth.

gross_margins.detail: gross_margins 97.78%, near-pure-toll network economics, well above 65%.

ai_exposure.detail: AI-driven fraud scoring, tokenization and agentic/commerce payment rails expand transaction volume; Visa monetizes throughput regardless of who builds the AI. Net tailwind, low disruption risk to the core toll.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Asset-light model (FCF $20.8B vs revenue $43B); capital intensity is negligible.

catalysts_general: Trading ~19% below mean analyst target ($398.83) and ~10% off 52w high.
Forward P/E 21.84 vs trailing 28.28 — earnings expected up ~29% (forward EPS $14.86 vs trailing $11.48).
Short interest fell 7.65% MoM; very low 1.29% float short.
Analyst tilt strongly positive: recommendation_mean 1.33, 36 buy-side of 39.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Consumer-spend slowdown trims forward multiple to 20x forward EPS $14.86.
Recession/regulatory (interchange) de-rate to 18x; near 52w-low support.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Below the 50-day (319.5) toward the lower 52w third; ~20x forward earnings — accumulation zone for a compounder of this quality.

comparables.note: Mastercard, closest direct peer; typically richer multiple than V.
American Express, credit-exposed model, cheaper but lower-margin.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Highest-quality name in the set: 97.8% gross margin, 60% ROE, real operating leverage, and the cleanest balance sheet relative to its toll-network model. Growth is only borderline (17%), but at 22x forward with ~19% upside to consensus and declining short interest, the risk/reward favors accumulation. BUY on weakness toward $300.

growth_picks.name: Tapestry, Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: revenue_growth 21.2% — mid-band, strong for luxury but not >30%.

capital_efficiency.detail: return_on_equity 60.91%, though flattered by a thin/levered equity base (D/E 575).

short_dynamics.detail: short_interest 9.0% (<10%) but RISING +15.17% — qualifies as borderline on the <10% leg only.

ai_exposure.detail: Luxury accessories demand is brand- and consumer-driven; AI helps at the margin (demand forecasting, personalization, marketing efficiency) but is neither a core driver nor a threat.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. High debt_to_equity (574.9) post-buybacks is the key balance-sheet flag.

catalysts_general: Price nearly doubled off the 52w low ($80.35) and sits 3.7% below the high — extended (+18.9% in 20d).
PEG 0.30 looks cheap, but driven by one-off earnings_growth (73.7%); forward P/E 20.1 vs trailing 47.7.
Rising short interest (+15.17%) into a vertical move — squeeze fuel or smart-money skepticism.
Mean target $164.47 implies only ~5% upside; the move has largely played out.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Multiple normalizes to 16x forward EPS $7.74 as earnings-growth base effect fades.
China luxury softness + consumer pullback de-rates to 14x.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Back toward the 50-day (142.6) and below; chasing within 4% of the 52w high after a near-double is poor risk/reward.

comparables.note: In-input peer; similar multiple, slower revenue growth, also extended.
Capri Holdings, the failed-merger counterparty; cheaper, weaker brands.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Genuinely strong execution — Coach-led margin expansion drove 74% earnings growth — and the franchise quality is real. But the stock has nearly doubled off its low, sits within 4% of its 52w high with only ~5% to consensus target, and short interest is climbing into the move. Quality name, wrong entry; WATCHLIST for a pullback toward $130.

growth_picks.name: Datadog, Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: revenue_growth 32.2%, above the 30% bar — best top-line in the set.

rule_of_40.detail: 32.2 + 0.80 (operating_margins 0.80%) = 33.0 — clears 30 only because growth carries near-zero GAAP margin.

earnings_accel.detail: earnings_growth 104% > revenue_growth 32.2% — leverage emerging off a tiny base.

capital_efficiency.detail: return_on_equity 3.93%, below 10% — profitability still immature (trailing EPS $0.39).

ai_exposure.detail: LLM observability and AI-agent monitoring are direct new SKUs; more AI workloads in production means more telemetry to monitor. Among the clearest AI volume tailwinds in the set.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Net-cash ($4.76B cash vs $1.29B debt); the risk is valuation, not the balance sheet.

catalysts_general: Up 98.4% in 50 days and 79.3% in 90 days — a vertical move now 17% off the 52w high.
Forward P/E 80.7 (trailing 589.7) and price/sales 22.3 — priced for sustained hypergrowth.
Mean target $233.06 is essentially the current price — no consensus upside left.
Declining short interest (-8.38%) and strong sell-side (rec_mean 1.45) corroborate momentum.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Growth-multiple compression to 50x forward EPS $2.85 on any cloud-spend deceleration.
Rate/risk-off de-rate to 35x; round-trips toward the 52w low ($98).

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near the 50-day (179.4), pre-spike base; at ~63x forward the valuation risk is lower without abandoning the AI-observability thesis.

comparables.note: CrowdStrike — comparable hypergrowth SaaS multiple.
Snowflake — similar data-platform consumption model and rich valuation.

growth_picks.analyst_take: The best business model in the set — 32% growth, 80% gross margin, a real AI-observability tailwind, and net cash. The problem is the entry: it has doubled in 50 days, trades at 80x forward with zero upside to consensus target, and GAAP profitability (3.9% ROE) is still nascent. Right thesis, blown-out price; WATCHLIST for a base-build back toward $180.

growth_picks.name: Caterpillar, Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: revenue_growth 22.2% — strong for a cyclical industrial, mid-band.

gross_margins.detail: gross_margins 28.6%, below 50% — structural for heavy-machinery manufacturing.

earnings_accel.detail: earnings_growth 30.2% > revenue_growth 22.2% — operating leverage at cycle peak.

capital_efficiency.detail: return_on_equity 51.33%, very high (aided by leverage, D/E 230.8).

ai_exposure.detail: Indirect: AI/data-center buildout lifts demand for power-gen engines and infrastructure equipment, a modest demand tailwind, but AI does not transform the core machinery business.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Capital-intensive, cyclical; FCF $3.78B is thin relative to $431B market cap, and the print likely reflects cycle-peak earnings.

catalysts_general: Trading 1% below 52w high after +33.5% in 90d — fully extended on a cyclical.
Mean target $944.10 ≈ current price; no consensus upside.
Forward P/E 31.1 is rich versus historical mid-cycle industrial multiples (~15-18x); PEG 2.16.
Recommendation_mean 2.07 (hold-leaning), with 11 holds of 28 — the least bullish sell-side in the set.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Multiple reverts toward 25x forward EPS $30.10 as cyclical peak fears build.
Construction/mining capex rolls over; cyclical de-rate to 20x.

ideal_entry.reasoning: A pullback below the 50-day (859.6) toward ~26x forward; buying a cyclical at the 52w high into peak margins is the wrong side of the trade.

comparables.note: Deere — comparable heavy-equipment cyclical, cheaper multiple.
Komatsu — global mining/construction peer, materially cheaper.

growth_picks.analyst_take: A high-ROE, well-run industrial — but this is a cyclical trading at a cycle-peak 31x forward, pinned to its 52w high, with the mean target offering zero upside and the most cautious sell-side in the group. Low gross margins and a leveraged balance sheet add little cushion. AVOID at this level; reconsider only on a cyclical pullback toward $800.

growth_picks.name: Amazon.com, Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: revenue_growth 16.6% on a ~$743B base — strong absolute, borderline by the rule.

gross_margins.detail: gross_margins 50.6% — blended retail+AWS, just into the 50-65% band.

earnings_accel.detail: earnings_growth 74.8% >> revenue_growth 16.6% — AWS/ads margin mix driving leverage.

short_dynamics.detail: short_interest 0.94% (<10%, in fact <5%) but RISING +14.71% — borderline on the <10% leg, not declining.

ai_exposure.detail: AWS is a primary AI infrastructure beneficiary (Bedrock, Trainium, compute demand); AI also lifts ads targeting and logistics efficiency. Among the strongest structural AI tailwinds in the set.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Note: heavy AI/data-center capex is a real cash drag — FCF $9.8B is compressed against $743B revenue, the key thing to watch even though no capex_signals field was provided.

catalysts_general: Pulled back -6.8% in 20d and sits below both 20/50-day SMAs (~256) — buying weakness, not strength.
Trading ~21% below mean target ($312.51) — the largest consensus upside in the set.
Forward P/E 25.1 vs trailing 32.8 — earnings inflecting (forward EPS $9.86 vs trailing $7.54).
Broadest, most bullish coverage: 63 analysts, rec_mean 1.34, zero sells.
Short interest rising +14.71% but absolute level trivial (0.94%).

downside_scenarios.scenario: AI-capex margin worries compress to 22x forward EPS $9.86.
Broad tech de-rate to 20x; retests the 52w low ($196).

ideal_entry.reasoning: Toward 52w-low support and ~22x forward; current pullback already offers a reasonable entry, with $220 the higher-conviction add zone.

comparables.note: Closest AI-cloud comparable; AMZN cheaper on forward P/E.
Megacap AI/ads peer, similar valuation zip code.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Scores only 3.5 on a pure-growth rubric because blended retail drags the Rule-of-40 and margins — but that understates a 24% ROE business with 75% earnings growth and a top-tier AI/AWS tailwind, now on sale after a 7% pullback below its moving averages. With ~21% upside to consensus and the broadest bullish coverage in the set, the risk/reward is the best here. BUY, accumulating toward $220; watch AI capex on free cash flow.

growth_picks.name: Ralph Lauren Corporation

revenue_growth.detail: revenue_growth 16.6% — respectable for premium apparel, mid-band.

gross_margins.detail: gross_margins 69.87%, strong brand pricing power above 65%.

short_dynamics.detail: short_interest 10.51% AND rising +10.63% — fails both legs (>10% and increasing).

ai_exposure.detail: Premium-brand demand is heritage/consumer-driven; AI offers marginal efficiency in demand planning and personalization but is neither a driver nor a threat to the franchise.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Notably illiquid (avg vol ~648k shares) — thin float amplifies both squeeze and downside moves; held_pct_institutions reported >100% (1.08), consistent with heavy short-borrow.

catalysts_general: Pinned to the 52w high (0.62% below) after +28.6% in 20d — sharply extended.
Rising short interest (10.51%, +10.63%) into a low-liquidity name — squeeze risk both ways.
Mean target $428.00 implies only ~2% upside; the run has front-run consensus.
Forward P/E 20.66 vs trailing 27.86 — earnings expected up ~35% (forward EPS $20.38).

downside_scenarios.scenario: Multiple eases to 18x forward EPS $20.38 as the momentum unwinds.
Discretionary/luxury slowdown de-rates to 15x toward mid-range support.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Back toward the 20/50-day (~366-370); the >10% rising short base plus thin liquidity at the 52w high makes chasing here hazardous.

comparables.note: In-input peer; similar multiple, faster revenue growth and earnings inflection.
Capri — cheaper accessible-luxury peer, weaker brand momentum.

growth_picks.analyst_take: A high-margin, 35%-ROE brand with genuine earnings momentum — but the stock is glued to its 52w high after a 28% one-month surge, short interest above 10% and rising into a thinly traded float, with barely 2% left to consensus target and a sub-30 Rule-of-40. Quality is not in question; the entry is. WATCHLIST for a pullback toward $360.
